original_poem:
ఆకాశ వదనంలో

అందాల తిలకమా

చుక్కలన్ని రేకులై

విచ్చుకున్న పారిజాతమా

మబ్బులన్ని ముద్దులిడే

చిన్నారి ముద్దుగుమ్మ

వెన్నెల పాదాలపై

వెండి మువ్వవమ్మా ఆమరియుజాబిలమ్మా

ఈ తూరుపు తెలవారుతూ నిన్నే దాచేసిందా

మా చూపులకందని దివిలొ నిదురబుచ్చినదా

లేదా

రేయికి రేయికి నడుమ దాగుడుమూతలు ఆడి నీకై మేము వెతకాలని.. వేచి చూడాలనీ వెళ్ళి దాక్కుంటున్నావా... ఓ తుంటరి వలువా...!!!
translated_poem:
In Akasha Vadaman

Andala Tilakama

Petals as many drops

Parijatama who has been released

Kissing all the clouds

Baby girl

On the feet of the moon

Wendy is the third motherJabilamma

Did you hide this mistake knowing it?

Did the gods sigh that we did not see them?

Or

Are you playing hide-and-seek with Reiki Reiki and waiting for us to look for you?

preprocessing.alignment_note: Source and translation stanza counts match.

translated_lines: On the feet of the moon
Did you hide this mistake knowing it?
Did the gods sigh that we did not see them?

source_lines: రేయికి రేయికి నడుమ దాగుడుమూతలు ఆడి నీకై మేము వెతకాలని.. వేచి చూడాలనీ వెళ్ళి దాక్కుంటున్నావా... ఓ తుంటరి వలువా...!!!
translated_lines: Are you playing hide-and-seek with Reiki Reiki and waiting for us to look for you?
On the feet of the moon
Did you hide this mistake knowing it?
Did the gods sigh that we did not see them?

source_lines: రేయికి రేయికి నడుమ దాగుడుమూతలు ఆడి నీకై మేము వెతకాలని.. వేచి చూడాలనీ వెళ్ళి దాక్కుంటున్నావా... ఓ తుంటరి వలువా...!!!
translated_lines: Are you playing hide-and-seek with Reiki Reiki and waiting for us to look for you?

claude.raw_text: {"recitation_style":"reflective","emotional_arc":"The poem opens with cosmic wonder at the night sky, personifying it as a celestial face adorned with stars as flower petals, then shifts through tender address to the moon as a playful child, before closing in gentle grief and longing as dawn hides the moon, leaving the speakers waiting and searching across the boundary of night and day.","stanzas":[{"index":1,"emotion":"wonder","tone":"wonder","translation_quality":"partial","loss_note":"ఆకాశ వదనంలో means 'in the face of the sky'; translation renders it as a proper name, losing the personification of sky as a face.","metaphor_spans":[{"source_term":"ఆకాశ వదనంలో","abstract_meaning":"The sky conceived as a living face, a cosmic countenance within which celestial phenomena appear as features."}],"visual_motifs":["the vast open sky imagined as a human face gazing downward"]},{"index":2,"emotion":"wonder","tone":"wonder","translation_quality":"lost","loss_note":"అందాల తిలకమా means 'O beautiful tilaka mark'; the translation leaves it untranslated and loses the cosmetic-devotional metaphor of the moon as a forehead ornament.","metaphor_spans":[{"source_term":"అందాల తిలకమా","abstract_meaning":"The moon addressed as a beautiful tilaka dot on the forehead of the sky-face, marking it as sacred and adorned."}],"visual_motifs":["a luminous tilaka mark gleaming at the center of a dark celestial forehead"]},{"index":3,"emotion":"wonder","tone":"wonder","translation_quality":"partial","loss_note":"చుక్కలన్ని రేకులై means 'all the stars having become petals'; translation reverses the order and loses the transformation metaphor.","metaphor_spans":[{"source_term":"చుక్కలన్ని రేకులై","abstract_meaning":"Stars collectively transformed into flower petals, rendering the night sky as a blooming flower."}],"visual_motifs":["countless stars arranged like the petals of a flower spread across the night sky"]},{"index":4,"emotion":"wonder","tone":"wonder","translation_quality":"partial","loss_note":"విచ్చుకున్న పారిజాతమా means 'O Parijata flower that has bloomed open'; translation says 'released' rather than 'bloomed', losing the specific floral unfolding.","metaphor_spans":[{"source_term":"విచ్చుకున్న పారిజాతమా","abstract_meaning":"The star-filled sky addressed as a fully bloomed Parijata flower, invoking its mythological celestial origin and nocturnal fragrance."}],"visual_motifs":["a fully opened Parijata blossom whose white petals are the stars of the night sky"]},{"index":5,"emotion":"tenderness","tone":"tenderness","translation_quality":"faithful","loss_note":"","metaphor_spans":[{"source_term":"మబ్బులన్ని ముద్దులిడే","abstract_meaning":"Clouds personified as beings that kiss the moon, expressing intimate celestial affection."}],"visual_motifs":["soft clouds drifting close to the moon and pressing against it like gentle kisses"]},{"index":6,"emotion":"tenderness","tone":"tenderness","translation_quality":"partial","loss_note":"చిన్నారి ముద్దుగుమ్మ means 'tiny darling little one' with strong affectionate diminutive force; 'baby girl' captures tenderness but loses the specific Telugu endearment register.","metaphor_spans":[{"source_term":"చిన్నారి ముద్దుగుమ్మ","abstract_meaning":"The moon addressed as a beloved small child, framing the celestial body within a domestic register of parental or communal affection."}],"visual_motifs":["the moon imagined as a small cherished child cradled among clouds"]},{"index":7,"emotion":"peace","tone":"whisper","translation_quality":"partial","loss_note
